title: Optically variable device having tonal effect
abstract: The invention is directed to a method of producing a micro-structure for an optically variable device. The micro-structure has a plurality of micro-structure pixels. The method comprises determining the tonal range of an image, the image having a plurality of image pixels; assigning a tonal value for one or more image pixels based on the tonal range; determining a height value of each micro-structure pixel based on the tonal value of a corresponding image pixel or corresponding group of image pixels; and forming each micro-structure pixel at the determined height value to create the micro-structure.
claims:
Claims The claims defining the invention are as follows: 1. A method of producing a micro-structure for an optically variable device, the micro-structure having a plurality of micro-structure pixels, the method comprising determining a tonal range of an image, the image having a plurality of image pixels; assigning a tonal value for one or more image pixels based on the tonal range; determining a height value of each micro-structure pixel based on the tonal value of a corresponding image pixel or corresponding group of image pixels; and forming each micro-structure pixel at the determined height value to create the micro-structure. 2. A method of claim 1 , wherein the step of determining a height value includes determining a height value based on a look-up table. 3. A method of claim 1 , wherein the step of determining a height value includes determining a height value based on a linear or a non-linear function of the tonal value of one or more corresponding image pixels. 4. A method according to claim 1 , wherein the step of determining a height value includes determining a height value that is directly proportional to the tonal value of one or more corresponding image pixels, or directly proportional to a complementary tonal value of the tonal value of the one or more corresponding image pixels. 5. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the step of determining a height value includes determining a height value from a range of at least three discrete height values. 6. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the step of determining a height value includes determining a height value between 2 to 15 microns, or 2 to 12 microns. 7. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the optically variable device is one of an interlaced type integral image device, a projection type integral image device. 8. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the step of forming each micro-structure pixel includes forming the micro-structure pixels using an embossing process or micro-structure printing process. 9. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the step of forming each micro-structure pixel includes forming a micro-structure mould on an embossing roller or shim or on a micro-structure printing roller or shim. 10. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the step of forming each micro-structure pixel includes forming the micro-structure pixels using radiation curable ink. 1 1. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the image is an 8-bit grayscale image having 256 different tonal values. 12. A method according to any one claims 1 to 11 , wherein a portion of the image includes a binary image having two tonal values. 13. A micro-structure of an optically variable device, the micro-structure being manufactured using a method according to any one of the claims 1 to 12. 14. An optically variable device comprising: an image layer carrying a micro-structure, the micro-structure having a plurality of micro-structure pixels, each micro-structure pixel having a height corresponding to a tonal effect associated with the pixel; a plurality of focusing elements; the plurality of focusing elements and the image layer being configured to project one or more viewpoint images based on the micro-structure, and 

 wherein the pixels of the micro-structure have three or more discrete height values to create a varying tonal effect in the viewpoint images. 15. An optical variable device of claim 14, wherein the height values are determined based on a look-up table. 16. An optically variable device of claim 14, wherein the micro-structure depicts one or more raw images, and the height values are determined based on a linear or a non-linear function of tonal values of corresponding image pixels in the raw images. 17. The optically variable device of claim 14, wherein the height values are directly proportional to the tonal values of the corresponding image pixels, or directly proportional to complementary tonal values of the tonal values of the corresponding image pixels. 18. An optically variable device according to any one of claims 14 to 17, wherein the height value is between 2 to 15, or 2 to 12 microns. 19. An optically variable device according to any one of claims 14 to 18, wherein the micro-structure pixels have at least three different discrete height values. 20. An optically variable device according to any one of claims 14 to 18, wherein the micro-structure pixels have less than or equal to 256 different discrete height values. 21. An optically variable device according to any one of claims 14 to 20, further including one or more opaque or semi-opaque or transparent or translucent layers for at least partially covering the micro-structure. 22. An optically variable device according to any one of claims 14 to 21 , wherein the focusing elements are a 1 D and/or a 2D array of focusing elements. 23. A method of producing a micro-structure for an optically variable device, the micro-structure being defined by a plurality of micro-structure coordinates, the method comprising 

 determining a tonal range of an image, the image having a plurality of image datasets for defining the image; assigning a tonal value for one or more image datasets based on the tonal range, determining a height value associated with each coordinate of the micro structure based on the tonal value of a corresponding image dataset or corresponding group of image datasets, forming a micro-structure based on the height values determined for each coordinate. 24. A method of claim 22, wherein the image datasets include image pixels for defining the image. 25. A method of claim 23, wherein the image datasets include image vectors for defining the image.
